title: Recruitment Violations Penalties and Preventive Actions
content:
Recruitment violations are classified with corresponding penalties as follows:

| CLASSIFICATION   | 1st OFFENSE              | 2nd OFFENSE                             | 3rd OFFENSE                         | 4th OFFENSE                |
|------------------|-------------------------|--------------------------------------|------------------------------------|-------------------------|
| Serious          | Cancellation of license |                                      |                                    |                         |
| Less Serious     | 2 to 6 months suspension | 6 months to 1 day to 1 year suspension | Cancellation of license            |                         |
| 24617            | Reprimand               | 1 to 3 months suspension               | 3 months & 1 day to 6 months suspension | Cancellation of license |

(Refer to Table 1 for the list of offenses and their corresponding penalties.)

Preventive actions pending investigation against recruitment agencies include:

1. Suspension of Documentary Processing: The Administration may suspend processing of documents of a respondent agency for violation of any provision of these Rules, Orders, and regulations, without prejudice to the outcome of the investigation.

2. Preventive Suspension: Pending investigation, the license of the respondent agency may be suspended for a period not exceeding the imposable penalties under the revised schedule of penalties, on grounds including:
  a. Reasonable grounds to believe continued operation will lead to further violations or exploitation or adversely affect friendly relations or national interest.
  b. Prima facie evidence of violation of the Labor Code, its implementing rules and regulations, POEA Rules and Regulations, or any issuance of the Administration with strong evidence of guilt.
